refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR specifies a single obligation: ensuring teleoperation communication quality meets a defined packet loss limit. It is focused on what the system must achieve (support teleoperation with specified communication quality) without specifying how to achieve it, thus maintaining solution neutrality. There are no multiple obligations combined with 'and' or 'or' that require splitting.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the packet loss rate during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify it does not exceed the customer-defined limit (e.g., ≤ 1%).
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation.
The NGHE solution shall ensure communication quality meets the customer-defined packet loss limit.

candidate_dps: Implement adaptive communication protocol to dynamically adjust transmission parameters based on network conditions
Incorporate error detection and correction mechanisms to maintain packet loss within limits
Design redundant communication paths to ensure data delivery despite packet loss
Utilize data prioritization and compression to optimize bandwidth and reduce packet loss
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement adaptive communication protocol to dynamically adjust transmission parameters based on network conditions

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on a logical approach to maintain communication quality independently by adapting to network conditions, minimizing coupling with other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Network conditions are sufficiently monitored and reported to enable adaptive adjustments
Adaptive protocols can meet the strict packet loss requirements under all agreed network conditions
Operational policies and safety plans do not restrict dynamic communication adjustments
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including monitoring of network conditions as a supporting functional requirement to ensure the adaptive protocol has necessary inputs for operation.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the network gateway and communication management subsystems responsible for handling network protocols, data transmission, and adaptive communication strategies.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bi-directional interface for sending and receiving data packets over the network, enabling dynamic adjustment of transmission parameters.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Outputs communication status and network condition metrics to other system modules for monitoring and control.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as adaptive communication protocol logically belongs to network management; minimal diffusion as most related functions are internal.
Vehicle module is broader and includes many unrelated functions; adaptive communication would diffuse into other vehicle subsystems.
Teleoperation uses communication but is more focused on control commands; embedding adaptive protocol here would cause diffusion.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety monitoring; adaptive communication protocol is outside its core scope, leading to poor containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for implementing the adaptive communication protocol because it directly manages network communication and can encapsulate dynamic transmission adjustments with minimal coupling to other modules.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on real-time network condition data from other modules may cause some interface complexity.
If network link interface changes, adaptive protocol implementation may require updates.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Implement adaptive communication protocol to dynamically adjust transmission parameters based on network conditions' directly addresses the FR of supporting teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit. The DP logically satisfies the functional requirement by adapting transmission to maintain packet loss within acceptable limits.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on real-time network condition data from other modules may introduce interface complexity, but the adaptive communication protocol is well-contained within the NGHE Solution module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high containment score (9) and low diffusion score (2), indicating strong cohesion and minimal diffusion risk. The adaptive communication protocol logically belongs here, encapsulating dynamic transmission adjustments with minimal coupling to other modules.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible by monitoring packet loss metrics output via the Telemetry interface and ensuring they meet the customer-defined target under agreed network conditions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The ownership candidate 'NGHE Solution' clearly defines the implementation boundary for the DP, with well-defined external interfaces (Network Link and Telemetry) supporting the adaptive communication protocol implementation.

recommended_revisions: Monitor and manage dependencies on real-time network condition data to minimize interface complexity and maintain modularity.
Establish robust interface versioning and change management for the Network Link interface to reduce impact on the adaptive communication protocol implementation if interface changes occur.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification and containment. The DP directly satisfies the FR, the IM module encapsulates the DP with minimal diffusion risk, and verification feasibility and ownership allocation are clear. Although there are some interface dependency risks, these are manageable with recommended revisions and do not warrant further decomposition at this stage. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining a clear and contained implementation is preferable to avoid complexity.

revise.why: To monitor and manage dependencies on real-time network condition data and establish robust interface versioning and change management for the Network Link interface, reducing interface complexity and maintaining modularity.

assumptions: The adaptive communication protocol within the NGHE Solution module can be effectively verified via telemetry under agreed network conditions.
Interface dependencies can be managed through versioning and change management without requiring further decomposition.
open_risks: Potential dependency on real-time network condition data may introduce interface complexity.
Changes in the Network Link interface could impact the adaptive communication protocol implementation if not properly managed.
evidence_needed: Verification results showing packet loss metrics meet customer-defined targets under test conditions.
Interface versioning and change management plans for the Network Link interface.
